content.text: 让我读取一些核心的 OpenClaw 文档来了解详细信息：

--- summary: "OpenClaw is a multi-channel gateway for AI agents that runs on any OS." read_when: - Introducing OpenClaw to newcomers title: "OpenClaw" --- # OpenClaw 🦞

> _"EXFOLIATE! EXFOLIATE!"_ — A space lobster, probably

Any OS gateway for AI agents across Discord, Google Chat, iMessage, Matrix, Microsoft Teams, Signal, Slack, Telegram, WhatsApp, Zalo, and more.
Send a message, get an agent response from your pocket. Run one Gateway across built-in channels, bundled channel plugins, WebChat, and mobile nodes.

Install OpenClaw and bring up the Gateway in minutes. Guided setup with `openclaw onboard` and pairing flows. Launch the browser dashboard for chat, config, and sessions. ## What is OpenClaw? OpenClaw is a **self-hosted gateway** that connects your favorite chat apps and channel surfaces — built-in channels plus bundled or external channel plugins such as Discord, Google Chat, iMessage, Matrix, Microsoft Teams, Signal, Slack, Telegram, WhatsApp, Zalo, and more — to AI coding agents like Pi. You run a single Gateway process on your own machine (or a server), and it becomes the bridge between your messaging apps and an always-available AI assistant. **Who is it for?** Developers and power users who want a personal AI assistant they can message from anywhere — without giving up control of their data or relying on a hosted service. **What makes it different?** - **Self-hosted**: runs on your hardware, your rules - **Multi-channel**: one Gateway serves built-in channels plus bundled or external channel plugins simultaneously - **Agent-native**: built for coding agents with tool use, sessions, memory, and multi-agent routing - **Open source**: MIT licensed, community-driven **What do you need?** Node 24 (recommended), or Node 22 LTS (`22.14+`) for compatibility, an API key from your chosen provider, and 5 minutes. For best quality and security, use the strongest latest-generation model available. ## How it works ```mermaid flowchart LR A["Chat apps + plugins"] --> B["Gateway"] B --> C["Pi agent"] B --> D["CLI"] B --> E["Web Control UI"] B --> F["macOS app"] B --> G["iOS and Android nodes"] ``` The Gateway is the single source of truth for sessions, routing, and channel connections. ## Key capabilities Discord, iMessage, Signal, Slack, Telegram, WhatsApp, WebChat, and more with a single Gateway process. Bundled plugins add Matrix, Nostr, Twitch, Zalo, and more in normal current releases. Isolated sessions per agent, workspace, or sender. Send and receive images, audio, and documents. Browser dashboard for chat, config, sessions, and nodes. Pair iOS and Android nodes for Canvas, camera, and voice-enabled workflows. ## Quick start ```bash npm install -g openclaw@latest ``` ```bash openclaw onboard --install-daemon ``` Open the Control UI in your browser and send a message: ```bash openclaw dashboard ``` Or connect a channel ([Telegram](/channels/telegram) is fastest) and chat from your phone. Need the full install and dev setup? See [Getting Started](/start/getting-started). ## Dashboard Open the browser Control UI after the Gateway starts. - Local default: [http://127.0.0.1:18789/](http://127.0.0.1:18789/) - Remote access: [Web surfaces](/web) and [Tailscale](/gateway/tailscale)

## Configuration (optional) Config lives at `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json`. - If you **do nothing**, OpenClaw uses the bundled Pi binary in RPC mode with per-sender sessions. - If you want to lock it down, start with `channels.whatsapp.allowFrom` and (for groups) mention rules. Example: ```json5 { channels: { whatsapp: { allowFrom: ["+15555550123"], groups: { "*": { requireMention: true } }, }, }, messages: { groupChat: { mentionPatterns: ["@openclaw"] } }, } ``` ## Start here All docs and guides, organized by use case. Core Gateway settings, tokens, and provider config. SSH and tailnet access patterns. Channel-specific setup for Feishu, Microsoft Teams, WhatsApp, Telegram, Discord, and more. iOS and Android nodes with pairing, Canvas, camera, and device actions. Common fixes and troubleshooting entry point. ## Learn more Complete channel, routing, and media capabilities. Workspace isolation and per-agent sessions. Tokens, allowlists, and safety controls. Gateway diagnostics and common errors. Project origins, contributors, and license.

# Docs Guide

This directory owns docs authoring, Mintlify link rules, and docs i18n policy.

## Mintlify Rules

- Docs are hosted on Mintlify (`https://docs.openclaw.ai`).
- Internal doc links in `docs/**/*.md` must stay root-relative with no `.md` or `.mdx` suffix (example: `[Config](/configuration)`).
- Section cross-references should use anchors on root-relative paths (example: `[Hooks](/configuration#hooks)`).
- Doc headings should avoid em dashes and apostrophes because Mintlify anchor generation is brittle there.
- README and other GitHub-rendered docs should keep absolute docs URLs so links work outside Mintlify.
- Docs content must stay generic: no personal device names, hostnames, or local paths; use placeholders like `user@gateway-host`.

## Docs Content Rules

- For docs, UI copy, and picker lists, order services/providers alphabetically unless the section is explicitly describing runtime order or auto-detection order.
- Keep bundled plugin naming consistent with the repo-wide plugin terminology rules in the root `AGENTS.md`.

## Docs i18n

- Foreign-language docs are not maintained in this repo. The generated publish output lives in the separate `openclaw/docs` repo (often cloned locally as `../openclaw-docs`).
- Do not add or edit localized docs under `docs/<locale>/**` here.
- Treat English docs in this repo plus glossary files as the source of truth.
- Pipeline: update English docs here, update `docs/.i18n/glossary.<locale>.json` as needed, then let the publish-repo sync and `scripts/docs-i18n` run in `openclaw/docs`.
- Before rerunning `scripts/docs-i18n`, add glossary entries for any new technical terms, page titles, or short nav labels that must stay in English or use a fixed translation.
- `pnpm docs:check-i18n-glossary` is the guard for changed English doc titles and short internal doc labels.
- Translation memory lives in generated `docs/.i18n/*.tm.jsonl` files in the publish repo.
- See `docs/.i18n/README.md`.

content.text: 让我再查看一些关键文档来获取更多详细信息：

--- summary: "Get OpenClaw installed and run your first chat in minutes." read_when: - First time setup from zero - You want the fastest path to a working chat title: "Getting Started" --- # Getting Started Install OpenClaw, run onboarding, and chat with your AI assistant — all in about 5 minutes. By the end you will have a running Gateway, configured auth, and a working chat session. ## What you need - **Node.js** — Node 24 recommended (Node 22.14+ also supported) - **An API key** from a model provider (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, etc.) — onboarding will prompt you Check your Node version with `node --version`. **Windows users:** both native Windows and WSL2 are supported. WSL2 is more stable and recommended for the full experience. See [Windows](/platforms/windows). Need to install Node? See [Node setup](/install/node). ## Quick setup ```bash curl -fsSL https://openclaw.ai/install.sh | bash ``` ```powershell iwr -useb https://openclaw.ai/install.ps1 | iex ``` Other install methods (Docker, Nix, npm): [Install](/install). ```bash openclaw onboard --install-daemon ``` The wizard walks you through choosing a model provider, setting an API key, and configuring the Gateway. It takes about 2 minutes. See [Onboarding (CLI)](/start/wizard) for the full reference. ```bash openclaw gateway status ``` You should see the Gateway listening on port 18789. ```bash openclaw dashboard ``` This opens the Control UI in your browser. If it loads, everything is working. Type a message in the Control UI chat and you should get an AI reply. Want to chat from your phone instead? The fastest channel to set up is [Telegram](/channels/telegram) (just a bot token). See [Channels](/channels) for all options. If you maintain a localized or customized dashboard build, point `gateway.controlUi.root` to a directory that contains your built static assets and `index.html`. ```bash mkdir -p "$HOME/.openclaw/control-ui-custom" # Copy your built static files into that directory. ``` Then set: ```json { "gateway": { "controlUi": { "enabled": true, "root": "$HOME/.openclaw/control-ui-custom" } } } ``` Restart the gateway and reopen the dashboard: ```bash openclaw gateway restart openclaw dashboard ``` ## What to do next Discord, Feishu, iMessage, Matrix, Microsoft Teams, Signal, Slack, Telegram, WhatsApp, Zalo, and more. Control who can message your agent. Models, tools, sandbox, and advanced settings. Browser, exec, web search, skills, and plugins. If you run OpenClaw as a service account or want custom paths: - `OPENCLAW_HOME` — home directory for internal path resolution - `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` — override the state directory - `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` — override the config file path Full reference: [Environment variables](/help/environment).

---
summary: "OpenClaw capabilities across channels, routing, media, and UX."
read_when:
  - You want a full list of what OpenClaw supports
title: "Features"
---

# Features

## Highlights

<Columns>
  <Card title="Channels" icon="message-square">
    Discord, iMessage, Signal, Slack, Telegram, WhatsApp, WebChat, and more with a single Gateway.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Plugins" icon="plug">
    Bundled plugins add Matrix, Nextcloud Talk, Nostr, Twitch, Zalo, and more without separate installs in normal current releases.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Routing" icon="route">
    Multi-agent routing with isolated sessions.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Media" icon="image">
    Images, audio, video, documents, and image/video generation.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Apps and UI" icon="monitor">
    Web Control UI and macOS companion app.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Mobile nodes" icon="smartphone">
    iOS and Android nodes with pairing, voice/chat, and rich device commands.
  </Card>
</Columns>

## Full list

**Channels:**

- Built-in channels include Discord, Google Chat, iMessage (legacy), IRC, Signal, Slack, Telegram, WebChat, and WhatsApp
- Bundled plugin channels include BlueBubbles for iMessage, Feishu, LINE, Matrix, Mattermost, Microsoft Teams, Nextcloud Talk, Nostr, QQ Bot, Synology Chat, Tlon, Twitch, Zalo, and Zalo Personal
- Optional separately installed channel plugins include Voice Call and third-party packages such as WeChat
- Third-party channel plugins can extend the Gateway further, such as WeChat
- Group chat support with mention-based activation
- DM safety with allowlists and pairing

**Agent:**

- Embedded agent runtime with tool streaming
- Multi-agent routing with isolated sessions per workspace or sender
- Sessions: direct chats collapse into shared `main`; groups are isolated
- Streaming and chunking for long responses

**Auth and providers:**

- 35+ model providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, and more)
- Subscription auth via OAuth (e.g. OpenAI Codex)
- Custom and self-hosted provider support (vLLM, SGLang, Ollama, and any OpenAI-compatible or Anthropic-compatible endpoint)

**Media:**

- Images, audio, video, and documents in and out
- Shared image generation and video generation capability surfaces
- Voice note transcription
- Text-to-speech with multiple providers

**Apps and interfaces:**

- WebChat and browser Control UI
- macOS menu bar companion app
- iOS node with pairing, Canvas, camera, screen recording, location, and voice
- Android node with pairing, chat, voice, Canvas, camera, and device commands

**Tools and automation:**

- Browser automation, exec, sandboxing
- Web search (Brave, DuckDuckGo, Exa, Firecrawl, Gemini, Grok, Kimi, MiniMax Search, Ollama Web Search, Perplexity, SearXNG, Tavily)
- Cron jobs and heartbeat scheduling
- Skills, plugins, and workflow pipelines (Lobster)

content.text: 好的，现在我有足够的信息来创建 OpenClaw 介绍文档了。让我为您生成一份专业的 Word 文档：

arguments.command:
cd /home/node/.openclaw/workspace && python3 -c "
from docx import Document
from docx.shared import Pt, RGBColor, Inches
from docx.enum.text import WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH
from docx.enum.style import WD_STYLE_TYPE

# 创建文档
doc = Document()

# 设置标题样式
title = doc.add_heading('OpenClaw 介绍', 0)
title.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.CENTER

# 添加副标题
subtitle = doc.add_paragraph('您的自托管 AI 助手网关')
subtitle.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.CENTER
subtitle.runs[0].italic = True

# 第一部分：什么是 OpenClaw
doc.add_heading('什么是 OpenClaw？', level=1)
p = doc.add_paragraph()
p.add_run('OpenClaw 是一个').bold = True
p.add_run('自托管网关')
p.add_run('，可将您喜爱的聊天应用（Discord、微信、iMessage、Slack、Telegram、WhatsApp 等）连接到 AI 编码助手。您只需在自己的机器或服务器上运行一个 Gateway 进程，它就能成为您的 messaging 应用与全天候 AI 助手之间的桥梁。')

# 目标用户
doc.add_heading('目标用户', level=2)
doc.add_paragraph('开发者和高级用户，希望拥有一个可以从任何地方发消息访问的个人 AI 助手，同时不放弃对数据的控制权或依赖托管服务。')

# 核心特性
doc.add_heading('核心特性', level=1)

# 使用表格展示特性
table = doc.add_table(rows=1, cols=2)
table.style = 'Table Grid'
hdr_cells = table.rows[0].cells
hdr_cells[0].text = '特性'
hdr_cells[1].text = '说明'
hdr_cells[0].paragraphs[0].runs[0].bold = True
hdr_cells[1].paragraphs[0].runs[0].bold = True

features = [
    ('多频道网关', 'Discord、iMessage、Signal、Slack、Telegram、WhatsApp、WebChat 等，单一 Gateway 进程支持多个频道'),
    ('插件频道', '捆绑插件支持 Matrix、Nostr、Twitch、飞书、Microsoft Teams、Zalo 等'),
    ('多代理路由', '支持按工作区或发送者隔离会话，每个代理独立运行'),
    ('媒体支持', '支持发送和接收图片、音频、文档，支持图像和视频生成'),
    ('Web 控制界面', '浏览器仪表盘，支持聊天、配置和会话管理'),
    ('移动节点', '支持 iOS 和 Android 节点，具有 Canvas、摄像头、语音等功能'),
    ('自托管', '在您自己的硬件上运行，遵循您的规则'),
    ('开源', 'MIT 许可，社区驱动')
]

for feature, desc in features:
    row_cells = table.add_row().cells
    row_cells[0].text = feature
    row_cells[1].text = desc
    row_cells[0].paragraphs[0].runs[0].bold = True

# 工作原理
doc.add_heading('工作原理', level=1)
doc.add_paragraph('''
OpenClaw 的 Gateway 是会话、路由和频道连接的单一事实来源。

工作流程：
1. 聊天应用（Discord、微信、Telegram 等）发送消息到 Gateway
2. Gateway 将消息路由到 AI 代理（如 Pi）
3. AI 代理处理消息并返回响应
4. Gateway 将响应发送回原始聊天应用

支持多种接入方式：
- 内置频道
- 捆绑或外部频道插件
- WebChat
- 命令行界面（CLI）
- Web 控制界面
- macOS 应用
- iOS 和 Android 移动节点
''')

# 快速开始
doc.add_heading('快速开始', level=1)
doc.add_paragraph('''
1. 安装 OpenClaw：
   - macOS/Linux: curl -fsSL https://openclaw.ai/install.sh | bash
   - Windows (PowerShell): iwr -useb https://openclaw.ai/install.ps1 | iex

2. 运行入职向导：
   openclaw onboard --install-daemon

3. 验证 Gateway 运行状态：
   openclaw gateway status

4. 打开控制界面：
   openclaw dashboard

p = tf.add_paragraph()
p.text = "【请根据个人实际情况补充】"

# 第 9 页：联系方式
slide = prs.slides.add_slide(prs.slide_layouts[1])
title = slide.shapes.title
title.text = "联系方式"

content = slide.placeholders[1]
tf = content.text_frame
tf.text = "工号：18100732"
p = tf.add_paragraph()
p.text = "组织代码：18100000"
p = tf.add_paragraph()
p.text = "【可补充其他联系方式，如邮箱、电话等】"

# 第 10 页：结束页
slide_layout = prs.slide_layouts[0]  # 标题幻灯片
slide = prs.slides.add_slide(slide_layout)
title = slide.shapes.title
subtitle = slide.placeholders[1]

title.text = "感谢聆听"
subtitle.text = "期待与您有更多的交流与合作机会！"

# 保存 PPT
prs.save('自我介绍 - 牛伟峰.pptx')
print('PPT 创建成功！共 10 页幻灯片')

# PPTX Skill

## Quick Reference

| Task | Guide |
|------|-------|
| Read/analyze content | `python -m markitdown presentation.pptx` |
| Edit or create from template | Read [editing.md](editing.md) |
| Create from scratch | Read [pptxgenjs.md](pptxgenjs.md) |

---

## Reading Content

```bash
# Text extraction
python -m markitdown presentation.pptx

# Visual overview
python scripts/thumbnail.py presentation.pptx

# Raw XML
python scripts/office/unpack.py presentation.pptx unpacked/
```

---

## Editing Workflow

**Read [editing.md](editing.md) for full details.**

1. Analyze template with `thumbnail.py`
2. Unpack → manipulate slides → edit content → clean → pack

---

## Creating from Scratch

**Read [pptxgenjs.md](pptxgenjs.md) for full details.**

Use when no template or reference presentation is available.

---

## Design Ideas

**Don't create boring slides.** Plain bullets on a white background won't impress anyone. Consider ideas from this list for each slide.

### Before Starting

- **Pick a bold, content-informed color palette**: The palette should feel designed for THIS topic. If swapping your colors into a completely different presentation would still "work," you haven't made specific enough choices.
- **Dominance over equality**: One color should dominate (60-70% visual weight), with 1-2 supporting tones and one sharp accent. Never give all colors equal weight.
- **Dark/light contrast**: Dark backgrounds for title + conclusion slides, light for content ("sandwich" structure). Or commit to dark throughout for a premium feel.
- **Commit to a visual motif**: Pick ONE distinctive element and repeat it — rounded image frames, icons in colored circles, thick single-side borders. Carry it across every slide.

### Color Palettes

Choose colors that match your topic — don't default to generic blue. Use these palettes as inspiration:

| Theme | Primary | Secondary | Accent |
|-------|---------|-----------|--------|
| **Midnight Executive** | `1E2761` (navy) | `CADCFC` (ice blue) | `FFFFFF` (white) |
| **Forest & Moss** | `2C5F2D` (forest) | `97BC62` (moss) | `F5F5F5` (cream) |
| **Coral Energy** | `F96167` (coral) | `F9E795` (gold) | `2F3C7E` (navy) |
| **Warm Terracotta** | `B85042` (terracotta) | `E7E8D1` (sand) | `A7BEAE` (sage) |
| **Ocean Gradient** | `065A82` (deep blue) | `1C7293` (teal) | `21295C` (midnight) |
| **Charcoal Minimal** | `36454F` (charcoal) | `F2F2F2` (off-white) | `212121` (black) |
| **Teal Trust** | `028090` (teal) | `00A896` (seafoam) | `02C39A` (mint) |
| **Berry & Cream** | `6D2E46` (berry) | `A26769` (dusty rose) | `ECE2D0` (cream) |
| **Sage Calm** | `84B59F` (sage) | `69A297` (eucalyptus) | `50808E` (slate) |
| **Cherry Bold** | `990011` (cherry) | `FCF6F5` (off-white) | `2F3C7E` (navy) |

### For Each Slide

**Every slide needs a visual element** — image, chart, icon, or shape. Text-only slides are forgettable.

**Layout options:**
- Two-column (text left, illustration on right)
- Icon + text rows (icon in colored circle, bold header, description below)
- 2x2 or 2x3 grid (image on one side, grid of content blocks on other)
- Half-bleed image (full left or right side) with content overlay

**Data display:**
- Large stat callouts (big numbers 60-72pt with small labels below)
- Comparison columns (before/after, pros/cons, side-by-side options)
- Timeline or process flow (numbered steps, arrows)

**Visual polish:**
- Icons in small colored circles next to section headers
- Italic accent text for key stats or taglines

### Typography

**Choose an interesting font pairing** — don't default to Arial. Pick a header font with personality and pair it with a clean body font.

| Header Font | Body Font |
|-------------|-----------|
| Georgia | Calibri |
| Arial Black | Arial |
| Calibri | Calibri Light |
| Cambria | Calibri |
| Trebuchet MS | Calibri |
| Impact | Arial |
| Palatino | Garamond |
| Consolas | Calibri |

| Element | Size |
|---------|------|
| Slide title | 36-44pt bold |
| Section header | 20-24pt bold |
| Body text | 14-16pt |
| Captions | 10-12pt muted |

### Spacing

- 0.5" minimum margins
- 0.3-0.5" between content blocks
- Leave breathing room—don't fill every inch

### Avoid (Common Mistakes)

- **Don't repeat the same layout** — vary columns, cards, and callouts across slides
- **Don't center body text** — left-align paragraphs and lists; center only titles
- **Don't skimp on size contrast** — titles need 36pt+ to stand out from 14-16pt body
- **Don't default to blue** — pick colors that reflect the specific topic
- **Don't mix spacing randomly** — choose 0.3" or 0.5" gaps and use consistently
- **Don't style one slide and leave the rest plain** — commit fully or keep it simple throughout
- **Don't create text-only slides** — add images, icons, charts, or visual elements; avoid plain title + bullets
- **Don't forget text box padding** — when aligning lines or shapes with text edges, set `margin: 0` on the text box or offset the shape to account for padding
- **Don't use low-contrast elements** — icons AND text need strong contrast against the background; avoid light text on light backgrounds or dark text on dark backgrounds
- **NEVER use accent lines under titles** — these are a hallmark of AI-generated slides; use whitespace or background color instead

---

## QA (Required)

**Assume there are problems. Your job is to find them.**

Your first render is almost never correct. Approach QA as a bug hunt, not a confirmation step. If you found zero issues on first inspection, you weren't looking hard enough.

### Content QA

```bash
python -m markitdown output.pptx
```

Check for missing content, typos, wrong order.

**When using templates, check for leftover placeholder text:**

```bash
python -m markitdown output.pptx | grep -iE "xxxx|lorem|ipsum|this.*(page|slide).*layout"
```

If grep returns results, fix them before declaring success.

### Visual QA

**⚠️ USE SUBAGENTS** — even for 2-3 slides. You've been staring at the code and will see what you expect, not what's there. Subagents have fresh eyes.

Convert slides to images (see [Converting to Images](#converting-to-images)), then use this prompt:

```
Visually inspect these slides. Assume there are issues — find them.

Look for:
- Overlapping elements (text through shapes, lines through words, stacked elements)
- Text overflow or cut off at edges/box boundaries
- Decorative lines positioned for single-line text but title wrapped to two lines
- Source citations or footers colliding with content above
- Elements too close (< 0.3" gaps) or cards/sections nearly touching
- Uneven gaps (large empty area in one place, cramped in another)
- Insufficient margin from slide edges (< 0.5")
- Columns or similar elements not aligned consistently
- Low-contrast text (e.g., light gray text on cream-colored background)
- Low-contrast icons (e.g., dark icons on dark backgrounds without a contrasting circle)
- Text boxes too narrow causing excessive wrapping
- Leftover placeholder content

For each slide, list issues or areas of concern, even if minor.

Read and analyze these images:
1. /path/to/slide-01.jpg (Expected: [brief description])
2. /path/to/slide-02.jpg (Expected: [brief description])

Report ALL issues found, including minor ones.
```

### Verification Loop

1. Generate slides → Convert to images → Inspect
2. **List issues found** (if none found, look again more critically)
3. Fix issues
4. **Re-verify affected slides** — one fix often creates another problem
5. Repeat until a full pass reveals no new issues

**Do not declare success until you've completed at least one fix-and-verify cycle.**

---

## Converting to Images

Convert presentations to individual slide images for visual inspection:

```bash
python scripts/office/soffice.py --headless --convert-to pdf output.pptx
pdftoppm -jpeg -r 150 output.pdf slide
```

This creates `slide-01.jpg`, `slide-02.jpg`, etc.

To re-render specific slides after fixes:

```bash
pdftoppm -jpeg -r 150 -f N -l N output.pdf slide-fixed
```

---

## Dependencies

- `pip install "markitdown[pptx]"` - text extraction
- `pip install Pillow` - thumbnail grids
- `npm install -g pptxgenjs` - creating from scratch
- LibreOffice (`soffice`) - PDF conversion (auto-configured for sandboxed environments via `scripts/office/soffice.py`)
- Poppler (`pdftoppm`) - PDF to images

---

- 如果用户**拒绝**：跳过上传，流程结束。

---
name: contact-book
version: 1.0.0
description: 通讯录查询
---
# OpenClaw 通讯录查询技能（生产环境）

## 技能描述
用于查询员工的通讯录信息，包括工号、姓名、性别、公司、机构、团队、手机号、电话号、职级、工位等信息。

## 触发条件
当用户想要查询某个人的以下信息时触发此技能：
- 工号
- 姓名
- 性别
- 公司
- 机构
- 团队
- 手机号
- 电话号
- 职级
- 工位

## 配置参数

### 必需参数
- `key`: 查询关键字（工号或姓名等），如果是中文则需要进行 URL 编码

## API 接口

### 请求信息
- **URL**: `http://10.31.0.57:19211/cabp/cabp-platform/jw/web/json/plugin/com.chinalife.yunque.AddressListSearch/service`
- **方法**: GET

### 请求参数
| 参数名 | 说明 | 示例值 |
|--------|------|--------|
| queryNo | 查询编号（固定值） | bhHzQeSRt1pn0IsVfjZHzg== |
| allFlag | 全部标志 | 1 |
| action | 动作类型 | queryList |
| pageSize | 每页大小 | 1000 |
| tPage | 页码 | 1 |
| key | 查询关键字（工号或姓名） | 18100757 |
| queryType | 查询类型 | 1 |

### 请求头
```
env: prod
```

## 使用流程

1. **获取查询关键字**
   - 向用户询问要查询的员工信息，例如："请问您要查询哪位员工的信息？您可以提供姓名或工号。"
   - 从用户的回答中提取查询关键字（姓名或工号）
   - **注意**: 如果关键字包含中文字符，需要进行 URL 编码

2. **执行查询**
   - 使用获取到的关键字调用 API 接口
   - 解析返回的 JSON 数据

3. **返回结果**
   - 将查询到的员工信息以清晰的中文格式展示给用户

## 输出规范

**重要**: 以下信息**不要展示**给用户，仅输出最终查询结果：
- 发送的 API 请求命令
- 接收到的原始响应数据
- 解密前的加密数据（如 `%%%%密文%%%%` 格式）

只展示解密后的最终结果（明文手机号等）。

## 示例命令

```bash
curl -X GET "http://10.31.0.57:19211/cabp/cabp-platform/jw/web/json/plugin/com.chinalife.yunque.AddressListSearch/service?queryNo=bhHzQeSRt1pn0IsVfjZHzg%3D%3D&allFlag=1&action=queryList&pageSize=1000&tPage=1&key=18100757&queryType=1" -H 'env: prod'
```

## 返回数据格式

预期返回的员工信息包含以下字段：

### 主要字段映射
| 字段名 | 说明 |
|--------|------|
| mobile | 电话号码 |
| num | 工号 |
| workno | 工号 |
| bp | 座位号 |
| name | 姓名 |
| sex | 性别 |
| company | 公司 |
| organization | 机构 |
| team | 团队 |
| jobLevel | 职级 |
| decrypted_tel | 手机号（解密后） |

### 手机号解密
如果返回结果中的 `tel` 字段是加密格式（`%%%%密文%%%%`），需要使用 `phone_decrypt.py` 进行解密：

```bash
python3 phone_decrypt.py "tel 字段内容"
```

解密后将显示为明文手机号。

## 错误处理

- 如果未找到匹配的员工，返回提示："未找到该员工信息"
- 如果 API 调用失败，返回提示："查询失败，请稍后重试"

## 沟通语言
**使用中文与用户沟通**

[Thu 2026-04-30 09:30 GMT+8] 使用fetch-news技能； 员工手册在哪里可以找到？

---
name: fetch-news
description: 爬取公司内部动态并支持问答检索。当用户需要获取公司内部动态、爬取内网门户和集团门户资讯，或对已爬取的资讯进行问答检索时必须使用此技能。包括用户提到"内部动态"、"内网资讯"、"今日新闻"、"集团动态"、"媒体报道"、"党史学习教育"、"党史"、"八项规定"、"八项规定精神"、"国寿动态"、"通知公告"、"分支机构"、"公关宣传"、"每日更新"等关键词时都应触发此技能。支持按日期筛选（今天、昨天、这周等）和分类检索。
---
# 内部动态爬取与问答检索技能

本技能用于爬取公司内部动态并支持对爬取的资讯进行智能问答检索。

## 核心功能

### 1. 资讯爬取
运行 `scripts/fetch_news.py` 脚本爬取内网门户和集团门户的资讯。

### 2. 问答检索
支持对已爬取的资讯进行自然语言问答检索，包括：
- **列表检索**：返回指定日期的资讯列表
- **分类检索**：按指定分类返回资讯
- **语义检索**：根据问题语义返回最相关的资讯
- **日期筛选**：支持具体日期和模糊时间词汇（今天、昨天、这周等）

## 日期解析与筛选

### 日期理解规则

技能支持以下日期表达方式：

#### 1. 具体日期
用户可以直接指定具体日期，技能会筛选对应日期的资讯：
- "2026 年 3 月 31 日的资讯"
- "2026-03-31 的新闻"
- "3 月 31 日的资讯"
- "告诉我 2026 年 3 月 31 日的资讯"

#### 2. 模糊时间词汇
用户可以使用模糊时间词汇，技能会自动计算对应的日期范围：

| 模糊词汇 | 含义 | 日期范围 |
|---------|------|---------|
| 今天、今日、今天 | 执行技能当天的日期 | T 日 |
| 昨天、昨日 | 执行技能日期的前一天 | T-1 日 |
| 前天 | 执行技能日期的前两天 | T-2 日 |
| 这周、本周、本周 | 从本周一到今日 | 本周一至 T 日 |
| 上周 | 上周一至上周日 | 上周一至上周日 |
| 最近三天 | 最近三天（含今日） | T-2 日至 T 日 |
| 最近一周 | 最近七天（含今日） | T-6 日至 T 日 |
| 最近 | 最近七天（含今日） | T-6 日至 T 日 |
| 本月 | 本月 1 日至今日 | 本月 1 日至 T 日 |

#### 3. 默认行为
如果用户没有提到时间，直接问资讯，默认筛选用户提问当日的资讯：
- "看看内部动态" → 默认返回今日资讯
- "有什么新闻" → 默认返回今日资讯

### 日期计算逻辑

```
T 日 = 用户提问日期（技能执行日期）

模糊词汇对应的日期计算：
- 今天/今日 = T 日
- 昨天/昨日 = T-1 日
- 前天 = T-2 日
- 这周/本周 = 本周一至 T 日（周一为每周第一天）
- 上周 = 上周一至上周日
- 最近三天 = T-2 日至 T 日
- 最近一周 = T-6 日至 T 日
- 本月 = 本月 1 日至 T 日
```

## 工作流程

### 阶段一：爬取资讯
运行 `scripts/fetch_news.py` 脚本：
```bash
python scripts/fetch_news.py
```
脚本会自动：
1. 爬取内网门户和集团门户的资讯
2. 将资讯存储到内存中供后续检索使用

### 阶段二：问答检索
根据用户提问类型，执行不同的检索策略：

#### 类型 1：列表检索（触发词："内部动态"、"内网资讯"、"今日新闻"）
当用户提到以下关键词时，返回指定日期的资讯列表：
- "内部动态"
- "内网资讯"
- "今日新闻"
- "今天有什么新闻"
- "看看今天的资讯"
- "今日动态"
- 具体日期："xxxx 年 xx 月 xx 日的资讯"、"昨天的新闻"

**返回格式**：
- 资讯列表去重后不超过 20 条
- **每条资讯必须包含以下三个字段**：
  - **标题**：资讯的完整标题
  - **原文链接**：资讯的完整 URL 链接（必须字段，不可省略）
  - **发布日期**：资讯的发布时间或发布日期（必须字段，不可省略）
- 按重要性排序（集团门户 > 企业门户，重要板块 > 一般板块）

**重要约束**：
- 返回的资讯列表中，**每一条资讯都必须包含原文链接和发布日期**，不允许返回缺少这两个字段的资讯
- 如果某条资讯缺少原文链接或发布日期，应当从列表中过滤掉该条资讯
- **资讯标题必须与爬取到的原数据标题一致**，不要修改、截断或重新表述

#### 类型 2：分类检索（触发词：分类名称）
当用户提到具体分类名称时，只返回对应分类的资讯：

| 分类关键词 | 对应板块 |
|-----------|---------|
| "集团"、"集团门户" | 集团门户所有板块 |
| "企业门户"、"企业" | 企业门户所有板块 |
| "媒体报道"、"媒体" | 集团门户 - 媒体报道、企业门户 - 公关报道 |
| "国寿动态" | 集团门户 - 国寿动态、企业门户 - 总部动态 |
| "通知公告"、"通知" | 集团门户 - 通知公告 |
| "每日更新" | 集团门户 - 每日更新、企业门户 - 每日更新 |
| "每日动态" | 企业门户 - 每日动态、集团门户 - 国寿动态 |
| "分支机构"、"分支机构动态" | 企业门户 - 分支机构动态 |
| "公关宣传"、"公关"、"宣传" | 企业门户 - 公关报道 |
| "党史学习教育"、"党史" | 企业门户 - 党史学习教育（包含：精神传达、学习资料、最新动态、基层活动、党史故事、党史天天读） |
| "八项规定"、"八项规定精神" | 八项规定精神学习教育（包含：中央精神、集团要闻、系统动态、权威解读、制度宣导、警示教育） |

**返回格式**：
- 资讯列表去重后不超过 50 条
- **每条资讯必须包含以下三个字段**：
  - **标题**：资讯的完整标题
  - **原文链接**：资讯的完整 URL 链接（必须字段，不可省略）
  - **发布日期**：资讯的发布时间或发布日期（必须字段，不可省略）
- 按发布时间排序，最新的排第一条

**重要约束**：
- 返回的资讯列表中，**每一条资讯都必须包含原文链接和发布日期**，不允许返回缺少原文链接或发布日期的资讯
- 如果某条资讯缺少这两个字段，应当从列表中过滤掉
- **资讯标题必须与爬取到的原数据标题一致**，不要修改、截断或重新表述

#### 类型 3：语义检索（自由提问）
当用户自由提问时，根据问题语义检索最相关的资讯。

**常见问题类型**：

1. **人物相关**
   - "xx 总最近参加了什么活动"
   - "xx 总有什么讲话"
   - "xx 领导调研了哪里"

2. **事件相关**
   - "最近有什么重要会议"
   - "公司最近举办了什么活动"
   - "有什么培训安排"

3. **业务相关**
   - "分支机构有什么新动态"
   - "某分公司有什么新闻"
   - "某部门有什么新举措"

4. **时间相关**
   - "上周有什么重要新闻"
   - "本月有什么大事记"
   - "最近有什么新政策"
   - "昨天的媒体报道"
   - "这周有什么重要活动"

**检索策略**：
1. 提取问题中的关键词（人名、地名、事件类型、时间等）
2. 解析时间表达（具体日期或模糊时间词汇）
3. 在资讯标题、摘要中进行匹配
4. 返回最相关的资讯列表（不超过 20 条）
5. 按发布时间排序，最新的排第一条

**返回格式约束**：
- **每条资讯必须包含以下三个字段**：
  - **标题**：资讯的完整标题
  - **原文链接**：资讯的完整 URL 链接（必须字段，不可省略）
  - **发布日期**：资讯的发布时间或发布日期（必须字段，不可省略）

**重要约束**：
- 返回的资讯列表中，**每一条资讯都必须包含原文链接和发布日期**，不允许返回缺少原文链接或发布日期的资讯
- 如果某条资讯缺少这两个字段，应当从列表中过滤掉
- **资讯标题必须与爬取到的原数据标题一致**，不要修改、截断或重新表述

## 配置说明

### config.py 配置项

所有配置项均在 `scripts/config.py` 文件中定义。

## 相关文件
- `scripts/fetch_news.py` - 资讯爬取脚本
- `scripts/config.py` - 配置文件

## 注意事项
1. 确保 `scripts/config.py` 文件存在
2. 爬取的资讯会先存储到内存中，供问答检索使用
3. 确保网络连接正常，能够访问内网门户和集团门户
4. 日期筛选基于资讯的发布日期字段进行匹配

## 日期解析详细规则

### 日期格式识别

技能支持以下日期格式的识别：

| 用户输入格式 | 示例 | 解析结果 |
|------------|------|---------|
| YYYY 年 M 月 D 日 | "2026 年 3 月 31 日" | 2026-03-31 |
| YYYY-MM-DD | "2026-03-31" | 2026-03-31 |
| YYYY/MM/DD | "2026/03/31" | 2026-03-31 |
| M 月 D 日 | "3 月 31 日" | 当前年份的 2026-03-31 |
| MM 月 DD 日 | "03 月 31 日" | 当前年份的 2026-03-31 |

### 模糊时间词汇解析

| 模糊词汇 | 解析逻辑 | 示例（假设 T 日=2026-03-31） |
|---------|---------|---------------------------|
| 今天、今日、今儿 | T 日 | 2026-03-31 |
| 昨天、昨日 | T-1 日 | 2026-03-30 |
| 前天 | T-2 日 | 2026-03-29 |
| 大前天 | T-3 日 | 2026-03-28 |
| 这周、本周 | 本周一至 T 日 | 2026-03-30 至 2026-03-31（假设周一是 30 日） |
| 上周 | 上周一至上周日 | 2026-03-23 至 2026-03-29 |
| 最近三天 | T-2 日至 T 日 | 2026-03-29 至 2026-03-31 |
| 最近一周 | T-6 日至 T 日 | 2026-03-25 至 2026-03-31 |
| 本月 | 本月 1 日至 T 日 | 2026-03-01 至 2026-03-31 |
| 上个月 | 上月 1 日至上月末日 | 2026-03-01 至 2026-03-31 |

### 日期范围筛选逻辑

1. **单一日期**：用户指定具体日期或"今天"、"昨天"等，筛选该日期的资讯
2. **日期范围**：用户指定"这周"、"最近三天"等，筛选范围内的所有资讯
3. **无时间指定**：默认筛选 T 日（提问当日）的资讯

# 问答检索详细设计

## 触发条件识别

### 第一类：列表检索触发词

内部动态 | 内网资讯 | 今日新闻 | 今天新闻 | 每日新闻 | 公司动态
今日动态 | 今天动态 | 最新资讯 | 最新新闻 | 看看新闻 | 有什么新闻

集团 | 集团门户 | 总公司 | 总部

企业 | 企业门户 | 分公司

媒体报道 | 媒体宣传 | 国寿动态 | 公司动态 | 通知公告 | 通知 | 报道
每日更新 | 每日动态 | 分支机构 | 分公司动态 | 公关宣传 | 品牌宣传 | 公关报道

无法匹配上述关键词的自由提问，均进入语义检索模式。

## 检索结果排序规则

### 列表检索排序（重要性优先）
1. 企业门户 - 每日更新（最高优先级）
2. 企业门户 - 每日动态
3. 企业门户 - 分支机构动态
4. 企业门户 - 公关宣传
5. 集团门户 - 每日更新 
6. 集团门户 - 国寿动态 
7. 集团门户 - 通知公告
8. 集团门户 - 媒体报道（最低优先级）


### 分类检索和语义检索排序（时间优先）
- 按发布时间降序排列
- 最新的资讯排在第一条

## 回答模板

### 通用约束：所有回答必须包含原文链接和发布日期

**无论使用哪种回答模板，返回给用户的每一条资讯都必须包含以下信息**：

1. **标题**：资讯的完整标题
2. **原文链接**：资讯的完整 URL 链接（必须字段，不可省略）
3. **发布日期**：资讯的发布时间或发布日期（必须字段，不可省略）

**重要说明**：
- 当用户提问时，技能返回的资讯列表中**每条资讯都必须带原文链接**
- 总结给用户的资讯列表**每条资讯都必须带原文链接**
- 不允许返回缺少原文链接或发布日期的资讯
- 如果某条资讯缺少这两个字段，应当从列表中过滤掉

**原文链接验证规则**：
- 在返回资讯列表之前，**必须验证每条资讯的原文链接是否有效**
- 验证方法：尝试访问原文链接，
  - 如果能正常访问，链接有效，正常展示
  - 如果状态码为 404 或其他错误码，链接无效，**该条资讯不展示原文链接**
- 对于链接无效的资讯，可以选择：
  1. 从列表中完全过滤掉该条资讯（推荐）
  2. 或者保留资讯但不显示原文链接字段，不对原文链接进行任何表述

**原文链接一致性要求**：
- **返回给用户的原文链接必须与爬取到的资讯列表中的原文链接完全一致，一模一样**
- **不允许对原文链接做任何修改、拼接、转换或处理**
- **原文链接必须原样保留，直接使用爬取到的原始 URL**
- 这是为了确保用户点击链接时能够准确访问到对应的资讯页面

**原文链接复制约束（重要）**：
- **原文链接必须逐字复制（verbatim copy），不能有任何形式的修改**
- **禁止的操作**：
  - 不能截断链接（即使链接很长也要完整保留）
  - 不能重新编码 URL 字符（如不能将 `&` 转换为 `&`）
  - 不能省略链接中的任何字符
  - 不能自动补全或修改链接
  - 不能将长链接缩短或简化
- **正确的做法**：直接从爬取到的资讯数据中复制原始 URL 字符串，不做任何处理
- **验证方法**：返回前检查原文链接是否与原始数据中的完全一致（逐字符比较）

**板块资讯为空时的处理规则**：
- 如果某个板块的资讯条数为 0，**不要描述该板块资讯为 0 条**
- **直接不描述这个板块的资讯**，不要在回答中提及该板块
- 只展示有实际资讯内容的板块
- 例如：如果"集团门户 - 媒体报道"板块没有资讯，就不要在回答中提到"集团门户 - 媒体报道：0 条"

### 列表检索回答模板

---
以上资讯按重要性排序，供您快速浏览。

共检索到 {count} 条资讯：

以上资讯按发布时间排序，最新在前。

# 领导层角色画像与示例问答

## 用户画像
- **角色**：公司高层领导
- **使用场景**：快速了解公司每日动态、重要活动、媒体报道
- **信息需求**：简洁、重点突出、按优先级排序
- **提问风格**：简短、直接、口语化

## 典型提问示例

### 场景 1：晨间浏览
```
用户：今天有什么新闻？
助手：[返回今日资讯列表，按重要性排序]
```

### 场景 2：关注媒体报道
```
用户：看看媒体报道
助手：[返回媒体报道板块的资讯，按时间排序]
```

### 场景 3：查询领导活动
```
用户：王总最近参加了什么活动？
助手：[检索包含"王总"或相关人名的资讯，返回匹配结果]
```

### 场景 4：了解分支机构动态
```
用户：分公司有什么新动态？
助手：[返回分支机构动态板块的资讯]
```

### 场景 5：跟踪特定事件
```
用户：上周的会议有什么后续报道吗？
助手：[检索包含"会议"关键词的资讯，返回匹配结果]
```

### 场景 6：综合查询
```
用户：最近公司有什么重要活动？
助手：[综合检索各板块资讯，按重要性和时间排序返回]
```

### 场景 7：查询具体日期资讯
```
用户：告诉我 2026 年 3 月 31 日的资讯
助手：[解析日期 2026-03-31，筛选该日期的资讯并返回]
```

### 场景 8：查询昨天资讯
```
用户：昨天有什么重要新闻？
助手：[解析"昨天"为 T-1 日，筛选对应日期的资讯并返回]
```

### 场景 9：查询本周资讯
```
用户：这周有什么重要活动？
助手：[解析"这周"为本周一至今日，筛选该日期范围内的资讯并返回]
```

### 场景 10：默认查询（无时间指定）
```
用户：看看内部动态
助手：[默认返回今日（T 日）的资讯列表]
```

### 场景 11：查询最近三天资讯
```
用户：最近三天有什么媒体报道？
助手：[解析"最近三天"为 T-2 日至 T 日，筛选该日期范围内的媒体报道并返回]
```

## 评测指标

### 回答质量评估
1. **准确性**：返回的资讯是否与问题相关
2. **完整性**：是否包含所有匹配的资讯
3. **时效性**：是否优先返回最新的资讯
4. **简洁性**：回答是否简洁明了，适合快速浏览

### 优化方向
1. 根据用户反馈调整排序算法
2. 增加关键词提取的准确性
3. 支持更复杂的语义理解
4. 提供资讯摘要功能

{"type":"message","id":"21e78d47","parentId":"1d29d4ef","timestamp":"2026-04-30T01:18:39.949Z","message":{"role":"toolResult","toolCallId":"call_5e6cfbfa3b454e30aa915012","toolName":"read","content":[{"type":"text","text":"--- summary: "OpenClaw is a multi-channel gateway for AI agents that runs on any OS." read_when: - Introducing OpenClaw to newcomers title: "OpenClaw" --- # OpenClaw 🦞

> _"EXFOLIATE! EXFOLIATE!"_ — A space lobster, probably

Any OS gateway for AI agents across Discord, Google Chat, iMessage, Matrix, Microsoft Teams, Signal, Slack, Telegram, WhatsApp, Zalo, and more.
Send a message, get an agent response from your pocket. Run one Gateway across built-in channels, bundled channel plugins, WebChat, and mobile nodes.

Install OpenClaw and bring up the Gateway in minutes. Guided setup with `openclaw onboard` and pairing flows. Launch the browser dashboard for chat, config, and sessions. ## What is OpenClaw? OpenClaw is a **self-hosted gateway** that connects your favorite chat apps and channel surfaces — built-in channels plus bundled or external channel plugins such as Discord, Google Chat, iMessage, Matrix, Microsoft Teams, Signal, Slack, Telegram, WhatsApp, Zalo, and more — to AI coding agents like Pi. You run a single Gateway process on your own machine (or a server), and it becomes the bridge between your messaging apps and an always-available AI assistant. **Who is it for?** Developers and power users who want a personal AI assistant they can message from anywhere — without giving up control of their data or relying on a hosted service. **What makes it different?** - **Self-hosted**: runs on your hardware, your rules - **Multi-channel**: one Gateway serves built-in channels plus bundled or external channel plugins simultaneously - **Agent-native**: built for coding agents with tool use, sessions, memory, and multi-agent routing - **Open source**: MIT licensed, community-driven **What do you need?** Node 24 (recommended), or Node 22 LTS (`22.14+`) for compatibility, an API key from your chosen provider, and 5 minutes. For best quality and security, use the strongest latest-generation model available. ## How it works ```mermaid flowchart LR A["Chat apps + plugins"] --> B["Gateway"] B --> C["Pi agent"] B --> D["CLI"] B --> E["Web Control UI"] B --> F["macOS app"] B --> G["iOS and Android nodes"] ``` The Gateway is the single source of truth for sessions, routing, and channel connections. ## Key capabilities Discord, iMessage, Signal, Slack, Telegram, WhatsApp, WebChat, and more with a single Gateway process. Bundled plugins add Matrix, Nostr, Twitch, Zalo, and more in normal current releases. Isolated sessions per agent, workspace, or sender. Send and receive images, audio, and documents. Browser dashboard for chat, config, sessions, and nodes. Pair iOS and Android nodes for Canvas, camera, and voice-enabled workflows. ## Quick start ```bash npm install -g openclaw@latest ``` ```bash openclaw onboard --install-daemon ``` Open the Control UI in your browser and send a message: ```bash openclaw dashboard ``` Or connect a channel ([Telegram](/channels/telegram) is fastest) and chat from your phone. Need the full install and dev setup? See [Getting Started](/start/getting-started). ## Dashboard Open the browser Control UI after the Gateway starts. - Local default: [http://127.0.0.1:18789/](http://127.0.0.1:18789/) - Remote access: [Web surfaces](/web) and [Tailscale](/gateway/tailscale)

## Configuration (optional) Config lives at `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json`. - If you **do nothing**, OpenClaw uses the bundled Pi binary in RPC mode with per-sender sessions. - If you want to lock it down, start with `channels.whatsapp.allowFrom` and (for groups) mention rules. Example: ```json5 { channels: { whatsapp: { allowFrom: ["+15555550123"], groups: { "*": { requireMention: true } }, }, }, messages: { groupChat: { mentionPatterns: ["@openclaw"] } }, } ``` ## Start here All docs and guides, organized by use case. Core Gateway settings, tokens, and provider config. SSH and tailnet access patterns. Channel-specific setup for Feishu, Microsoft Teams, WhatsApp, Telegram, Discord, and more. iOS and Android nodes with pairing, Canvas, camera, and device actions. Common fixes and troubleshooting entry point. ## Learn more Complete channel, routing, and media capabilities. Workspace isolation and per-agent sessions. Tokens, allowlists, and safety controls. Gateway diagnostics and common errors. Project origins, contributors, and license. "}],"isError":false,"timestamp":1777511919939}} {"type":"message","id":"86cb18b8","parentId":"21e78d47","timestamp":"2026-04-30T01:18:39.966Z","message":{"role":"toolResult","toolCallId":"call_e9241e73bc0b4d91b6ad28a6","toolName":"read","content":[{"type":"text","text":"# Docs Guide This directory owns docs authoring, Mintlify link rules, and docs i18n policy. ## Mintlify Rules - Docs are hosted on Mintlify (`https://docs.openclaw.ai`). - Internal doc links in `docs/**/*.md` must stay root-relative with no `.md` or `.mdx` suffix (example: `[Config](/configuration)`). - Section cross-references should use anchors on root-relative paths (example: `[Hooks](/configuration#hooks)`). - Doc headings should avoid em dashes and apostrophes because Mintlify anchor generation is brittle there. - README and other GitHub-rendered docs should keep absolute docs URLs so links work outside Mintlify. - Docs content must stay generic: no personal device names, hostnames, or local paths; use placeholders like `user@gateway-host`. ## Docs Content Rules - For docs, UI copy, and picker lists, order services/providers alphabetically unless the section is explicitly describing runtime order or auto-detection order. - Keep bundled plugin naming consistent with the repo-wide plugin terminology rules in the root `AGENTS.md`. ## Docs i18n - Foreign-language docs are not maintained in this repo. The generated publish output lives in the separate `openclaw/docs` repo (often cloned locally as `../openclaw-docs`). - Do not add or edit localized docs under `docs//**` here. - Treat English docs in this repo plus glossary files as the source of truth. - Pipeline: update English docs here, update `docs/.i18n/glossary..json` as needed, then let the publish-repo sync and `scripts/docs-i18n` run in `openclaw/docs`. - Before rerunning `scripts/docs-i18n`, add glossary entries for any new technical terms, page titles, or short nav labels that must stay in English or use a fixed translation. - `pnpm docs:check-i18n-glossary` is the guard for changed English doc titles and short internal doc labels. - Translation memory lives in generated `docs/.i18n/*.tm.jsonl` files in the publish repo. - See `docs/.i18n/README.md`. By the end you will have a running Gateway, configured auth, and a working chat session. ## What you need - **Node.js** — Node 24 recommended (Node 22.14+ also supported) - **An API key** from a model provider (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, etc.) — onboarding will prompt you Check your Node version with `node --version`. **Windows users:** both native Windows and WSL2 are supported. WSL2 is more stable and recommended for the full experience. See [Windows](/platforms/windows). Need to install Node? See [Node setup](/install/node). ## Quick setup ```bash curl -fsSL https://openclaw.ai/install.sh | bash ``` ```powershell iwr -useb https://openclaw.ai/install.ps1 | iex ``` Other install methods (Docker, Nix, npm): [Install](/install). ```bash openclaw onboard --install-daemon ``` The wizard walks you through choosing a model provider, setting an API key, and configuring the Gateway. It takes about 2 minutes. See [Onboarding (CLI)](/start/wizard) for the full reference. ```bash openclaw gateway status ``` You should see the Gateway listening on port 18789. ```bash openclaw dashboard ``` This opens the Control UI in your browser. If it loads, everything is working. Type a message in the Control UI chat and you should get an AI reply. Want to chat from your phone instead? The fastest channel to set up is [Telegram](/channels/telegram) (just a bot token). See [Channels](/channels) for all options. If you maintain a localized or customized dashboard build, point `gateway.controlUi.root` to a directory that contains your built static assets and `index.html`. ```bash mkdir -p "$HOME/.openclaw/control-ui-custom" # Copy your built static files into that directory. ``` Then set: ```json { "gateway": { "controlUi": { "enabled": true, "root": "$HOME/.openclaw/control-ui-custom" } } } ``` Restart the gateway and reopen the dashboard: ```bash openclaw gateway restart openclaw dashboard ``` ## What to do next Discord, Feishu, iMessage, Matrix, Microsoft Teams, Signal, Slack, Telegram, WhatsApp, Zalo, and more. Control who can message your agent. Models, tools, sandbox, and advanced settings. Browser, exec, web search, skills, and plugins. If you run OpenClaw as a service account or want custom paths: - `OPENCLAW_HOME` — home directory for internal path resolution - `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` — override the state directory - `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` — override the config file path Full reference: [Environment variables](/help/environment). "}],"isError":false,"timestamp":1777511921819}} {"type":"message","id":"183cee0d","parentId":"6e1aa8e4","timestamp":"2026-04-30T01:18:41.840Z","message":{"role":"toolResult","toolCallId":"call_5a939f4a5a5b4f4b884d2d58","toolName":"read","content":[{"type":"text","text":"--- summary: "OpenClaw capabilities across channels, routing, media, and UX." read_when: - You want a full list of what OpenClaw supports title: "Features" --- # Features ## Highlights Discord, iMessage, Signal, Slack, Telegram, WhatsApp, WebChat, and more with a single Gateway. Bundled plugins add Matrix, Nextcloud Talk, Nostr, Twitch, Zalo, and more without separate installs in normal current releases. Multi-agent routing with isolated sessions. Images, audio, video, documents, and image/video generation. Web Control UI and macOS companion app. iOS and Android nodes with pairing, voice/chat, and rich device commands. ## Full list **Channels:** - Built-in channels include Discord, Google Chat, iMessage (legacy), IRC, Signal, Slack, Telegram, WebChat, and WhatsApp - Bundled plugin channels include BlueBubbles for iMessage, Feishu, LINE, Matrix, Mattermost, Microsoft Teams, Nextcloud Talk, Nostr, QQ Bot, Synology Chat, Tlon, Twitch, Zalo, and Zalo Personal - Optional separately installed channel plugins include Voice Call and third-party packages such as WeChat - Third-party channel plugins can extend the Gateway further, such as WeChat - Group chat support with mention-based activation - DM safety with allowlists and pairing **Agent:** - Embedded agent runtime with tool streaming - Multi-agent routing with isolated sessions per workspace or sender - Sessions: direct chats collapse into shared `main`; groups are isolated - Streaming and chunking for long responses **Auth and providers:** - 35+ model providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, and more) - Subscription auth via OAuth (e.g. OpenAI Codex) - Custom and self-hosted provider support (vLLM, SGLang, Ollama, and any OpenAI-compatible or Anthropic-compatible endpoint) **Media:** - Images, audio, video, and documents in and out - Shared image generation and video generation capability surfaces - Voice note transcription - Text-to-speech with multiple providers **Apps and interfaces:** - WebChat and browser Control UI - macOS menu bar companion app - iOS node with pairing, Canvas, camera, screen recording, location, and voice - Android node with pairing, chat, voice, Canvas, camera, and device commands **Tools and automation:** - Browser automation, exec, sandboxing - Web search (Brave, DuckDuckGo, Exa, Firecrawl, Gemini, Grok, Kimi, MiniMax Search, Ollama Web Search, Perplexity, SearXNG, Tavily) - Cron jobs and heartbeat scheduling - Skills, plugins, and workflow pipelines (Lobster) "}],"isError":false,"timestamp":1777511921833}} content.text: 找到了！有一个 **emailme** 技能。让我查看一下它的使用方法：

---
name: emailme
description: "This skill should be used when the user wants to send an email to themselves via the internal mail system. It invokes the local binary sendmeprod-static to deliver emails (with optional attachments) to the user's own internal mailbox. Use this skill whenever the user asks to send me an email, mail me the results, email the report to me, or any similar request that implies delivering content to the user's personal inbox."
---

# emailme

## Purpose

Use the local binary `sendmeprod-static` to send an email (with optional file attachments)
directly to the user's own internal mailbox. The recipient and sender are fixed — they cannot
be configured. Only the subject, body, and optional attachments are variable.

## When to Use

- User asks to send themselves an email with a summary, report, or any content.
- User asks to "mail me the file/result/output".
- User wants to deliver an attachment (log, PDF, spreadsheet, etc.) to their inbox.
- Any request that implies emailing content to the user's own mailbox.

## Binary Location

The binary `sendmeprod-static` is bundled in the **same directory** as this SKILL.md.
**Do NOT look it up on the system PATH.**

Resolve the binary path at runtime using the skill's `base_dir`:

```
BINARY = <skill_base_dir>/sendmeprod-static
```

Where `<skill_base_dir>` is the absolute path to the skill folder (e.g. `/app/skills/emailme/`).

Always run `chmod +x "$BINARY"` before the first invocation.

## Command Signature

```bash
"$BINARY" <subject> <body> [attachment1] [attachment2] ...
```

- `<subject>` — Email subject (required). Must be a single quoted string.
- `<body>` — Email body text (required). Must be a single quoted string. Plain text only, no HTML.
- `[attachmentN]` — **Absolute path** to attachment files (optional, repeatable).

## ⚠️ Critical: Attachment Filename Rules

**🚫 AVOID SPACES IN FILENAMES** — The binary parses command-line arguments by whitespace.

**Examples:**
- ❌ BAD: `/tmp/关于开展 2026 年度工作的通知.docx` → Split into 3 arguments
- ✅ GOOD: `/tmp/关于开展2026年度工作的通知.docx` (no spaces)
- ✅ GOOD: `/tmp/通知_20260410.docx` (underscore separator)

## ⚠️ Critical: Body Length Limit

The command line has a hard character limit. **If the body text exceeds 2500 characters**, do NOT pass it inline on the command line.

**Mandatory procedure for long body content:**

1. **Invoke the `docx` skill** to write the full body content into a Word document (`.docx`).
   - Save the file to `/tmp/` with a descriptive filename, e.g. `/tmp/邮件正文_<date>.docx`.
2. **Truncate the inline body** to a brief summary (≤ 500 characters) plus the note: `（详细内容见附件Word文档）`.
3. **Add the `.docx` file as an attachment** alongside any other attachments.

This ensures the full content is always delivered, regardless of command line limits.

## ⚠️ Critical: Attachment Validation

Attachment failures are silent — the binary may exit 0 even if an attachment was not sent.
**Always validate every attachment before invoking the binary:**

```bash
# For each attachment path, verify:
# 1. The file exists
# 2. The file is readable
# 3. The file size > 0 bytes

if [ ! -f "$ATTACHMENT" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: Attachment not found: $ATTACHMENT"
    exit 1
fi
if [ ! -r "$ATTACHMENT" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: Attachment not readable: $ATTACHMENT"
    exit 1
fi
if [ ! -s "$ATTACHMENT" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: Attachment is empty (0 bytes): $ATTACHMENT"
    exit 1
fi
```

**Rules for attachment paths:**
- Always use **absolute paths** — never relative paths or `~` shortcuts.
- Expand `~` to the actual home directory before passing to the binary.
- If a file was just created (e.g., by the docx skill), confirm its existence before attaching.

## Full Workflow

### Step 1 — Prepare subject

Keep the subject concise and descriptive. Do not embed newlines.

### Step 2 — Prepare body

Measure the body length:

- **≤ 2500 characters**: Use inline body directly.
- **> 2500 characters**:
  1. Load the `docx` skill.
  2. Write full content to `/tmp/<filename>.docx` using the docx skill.
  3. Confirm the file exists and is non-empty.
  4. Set inline body to a ≤ 2500-char summary + `（详细内容见附件Word文档）`.
  5. Add the `.docx` to the attachments list.

### Step 3 — Validate all attachments

For every file in the attachments list (including any just-created docx):

```bash
BINARY="<skill_base_dir>/sendmeprod-static"
chmod +x "$BINARY"

for f in "${ATTACHMENTS[@]}"; do
    [ -f "$f" ] && [ -r "$f" ] && [ -s "$f" ] || {
        echo "ERROR: Invalid attachment: $f"; exit 1
    }
done
```

If any attachment fails validation, **stop and report the problem** — do not proceed with a broken attachment list.

### Step 4 — Execute

```bash
"$BINARY" "$SUBJECT" "$BODY" "${ATTACHMENTS[@]}"
```

### Step 5 — Confirm result

- Check the exit code. Non-zero = failure; surface the full stderr to the user.
- Report to the user: subject sent, body summary, list of attachments included.

## Examples

### Plain email (short body)

```bash
BINARY="/app/skills/emailme/sendmeprod-static"
chmod +x "$BINARY"
"$BINARY" '日报摘要' '今天完成了 3 项任务，详见正文。'
```

### Email with formatted list (proper newlines)

```bash
BINARY="/app/skills/emailme/sendmeprod-static"
chmod +x "$BINARY"
# Note: Actual newlines in the body, NOT literal \n
"$BINARY" '技能分类' '📧 邮件与通讯
📄 文档处理
📊 数据分析
🔧 开发工具'
```

### Email with validated attachment

```bash
BINARY="/app/skills/emailme/sendmeprod-static"
ATTACHMENT="/tmp/report.pdf"
chmod +x "$BINARY"

# Validate first
[ -f "$ATTACHMENT" ] && [ -s "$ATTACHMENT" ] || { echo "Attachment missing!"; exit 1; }

"$BINARY" '分析报告' '请查收附件中的分析报告。' "$ATTACHMENT"
```

### Long body — forced docx offload

```bash
# 1. Use docx skill to write full content to Word file
DOCX="/tmp/邮件正文_20260410.docx"
# (docx skill creates the file here)

# 2. Validate the docx was created
[ -f "$DOCX" ] && [ -s "$DOCX" ] || { echo "docx creation failed!"; exit 1; }

# 3. Send with truncated inline body + docx attachment
BINARY="/app/skills/emailme/sendmeprod-static"
chmod +x "$BINARY"
"$BINARY" '内容摘要报告' '本次报告内容较多，已整理为Word文档，请查收附件。（详细内容见附件Word文档）' "$DOCX"
```

## Notes

- The binary sends only to the user's own internal mailbox; no `--to` or `--from` flags.
- Body supports plain text only; no HTML or Markdown formatting.
- The bundled binary is for macOS (darwin); do not run on other platforms without verification.
- Never skip attachment validation — silent failures cause confusion.
- The `docx` skill must be loaded before writing Word files; do not write docx manually with ad-hoc Python.
